summary: Created `_bmad-output/planning-artifacts/data-ingestion-admin-epics-and-stories.md` with a constrained decomposition of the approved data-ingestion-admin scope into 3 user-value epics and 12 implementation-sized stories. The artifact keeps the MVP limited to indicator batch ingestion, carries a full FR/NFR inventory plus FR coverage map, and includes operational readiness work for rollback, runbook, and regression coverage. A narrow validation pass confirmed the file materialized and no template placeholders remained.

decisions.title: Epic shape
decisions.decision: Organized the work into 3 user-value epics: safe preview intake, controlled apply with backup/audit, and execution recovery/operational readiness.
decisions.rationale: This follows the BMAD rule to avoid technical-layer epics while still matching the approved architecture sequence.

decisions.title: Scope boundary
decisions.decision: Kept all stories inside the approved MVP boundary for student indicator initial-load batches only.
decisions.rationale: The architecture and project context explicitly reject generic repository-wide JSON ingestion during stabilization.

decisions.title: Primary source model
decisions.decision: Treated uploaded structured JSON as the official input source across preview/apply stories.
decisions.rationale: That is the architecture-approved source model and best fits the existing frontend/backend transport conventions.

decisions.title: Operational safety model
decisions.decision: Made preview-before-apply, revalidation, snapshot-before-write, all-or-nothing apply, and separate audit persistence first-class story outcomes.
decisions.rationale: These are the key safety requirements that distinguish this feature from the existing single-student indicator load flow.

decisions.title: Recovery and readiness placement
decisions.decision: Grouped execution detail lookup, rollback, runbook, and regression coverage into the final epic.
decisions.rationale: This keeps earlier epics independently valuable while reserving the stabilization and support concerns for a dedicated closing slice.

decisions.title: UX input handling
decisions.decision: Recorded that no standalone UX design specification was provided and derived only minimal UX constraints from the brief and architecture.
decisions.rationale: The provided inputs did not include a separate UX artifact, so no additional UX-driven story set was invented.

risks.risk: The BMAD workflow normally expects explicit interactive approval at requirements and epic checkpoints.
risks.mitigation: The artifact is marked as completed with approval required; review the 3-epic structure and story boundaries before implementation starts.

risks.risk: Implementation could drift back toward generic JSON ingestion if teams ignore the approved write-target boundary.
risks.mitigation: The artifact repeats the approved targets and ties them directly to Epic 2 acceptance criteria.

risks.risk: Rollback posture may expand if stakeholders later require a primary UI rollback action rather than operator-assisted recovery.
risks.mitigation: The stories keep rollback in backend/service and operational flow first, which can be expanded later without widening MVP scope now.
